deck_types.label: Hackathon / Demo Day Deck
deck_types.audience: hackathon judges, sponsor judges, demo day reviewers
deck_types.primary_goal: Show a working product quickly and prove prize/sponsor fit.
must_prove: the sponsor or track fit is explicit
the team understands the technical constraint

deck_types.label: Investor / VC Deck
deck_types.audience: VCs, institutional seed investors, demo day investors
deck_types.primary_goal: Make a venture-scale funding case with proof, market, business model, team, and ask.

deck_types.label: Angel Deck
deck_types.audience: angels, operators, small checks, founder-friendly early investors
deck_types.primary_goal: Explain the founder, product, early proof, and immediate funding need without overbuilding the VC narrative.
slide_sequence: founder story and why this problem
ask, terms, and use of funds
must_prove: there is some proof or sharp learning
the check directly unlocks a near-term milestone
red_flags: too much strategy and not enough founder/product proof

deck_types.label: Accelerator Deck
deck_types.audience: YC, Techstars, 500 Global, Antler, EF, Sequoia Arc, accelerator screeners
deck_types.primary_goal: Prove founder edge, speed, customer insight, product proof, and program fit.
red_flags: founder story does not explain edge
program target is just a brand wish

deck_types.label: Grant / Partner Deck
deck_types.audience: grant committees, ecosystem teams, strategic partners
deck_types.primary_goal: Show ecosystem value, implementation credibility, milestones, budget, and mutual benefit.
